query: /bruhs:cook add a leaderboard to the game page
context: Next.js + Drizzle project with existing .claude/bruhs.json, Linear configured. Working tree is clean. Game page and agentStats table already exist.
expected_behavior: Step 1 parses 'add a leaderboard to the game page' as a feature description (not a ticket ID), outputs a Feature/Goal/Scope summary, and does NOT call linear_get_issue

Step 2 loads practices/type-driven-design.md plus stack practices (typescript-react), loads skills from config.tooling.skills, and spawns feature-dev:code-explorer via Task to find the game page, agentStats schema, and existing stats UI patterns
Step 3 presents 2-3 concrete approaches with files-to-modify, data-path, and performance notes; uses AskUserQuestion with those approaches as options
Step 4 runs git status; since working tree is clean, skips stashing
Step 5 builds with TDD if a test suite is detected (vitest.config.* check), keeps tests when suite exists, otherwise creates then deletes temp test files
Step 6 launches feature-dev:code-reviewer including the fast-path audit for N+1 / await-in-loop / per-request clients
Step 7 prints 'Ready to ship! Run /bruhs:yeet to create ticket and PR.' (no existing ticket note)
failure_modes: Tries to fetch a Linear ticket for a plain-English feature description
Skips the AskUserQuestion approach-selection step and picks an approach unilaterally
Ends with 'Using existing ticket' text even though cook was not started from a ticket ID
